edges.description: Triggered autoreactive B-cell responses feed local CNS/CSF anti-NMDAR antibody production.
Locally produced antibodies bind extracellular NR1/GluN1 epitopes on neuronal NMDA receptors.
Reduced surface and postsynaptic NMDAR availability impairs NMDAR-dependent synaptic signaling.
NMDAR hypofunction maps to the characteristic encephalitis syndrome of psychiatric symptoms, seizures, movement disorder, coma, autonomic dysfunction, and hypoventilation.
Barrier dysfunction may facilitate antibody and immune-cell traffic that sustains the intrathecal immune compartment.

nodes.description: Blood-brain barrier function modulates access of antibodies and immune cells between peripheral and CNS compartments, and inflammatory cytokine findings support barrier involvement in anti-NMDAR encephalitis.

evidence.reference_title: Anti-NMDAR antibodies, the blood-brain barrier, and anti-NMDAR encephalitis.
evidence.snippet: The blood-brain barrier (BBB), which separates the brain from the peripheral circulatory system, is crucial for antibodies and immune cells to enter or exit the CNS.

evidence.reference_title: Anti-NMDAR antibodies, the blood-brain barrier, and anti-NMDAR encephalitis.
evidence.snippet: The findings of cytokines in this disorder support the involvement of the BBB.

nodes.description: Initial treatment uses corticosteroids, intravenous immunoglobulin, plasma exchange, or combinations of these therapies, often alongside tumor removal when a tumor is identified.

evidence.reference_title: Treatment and prognostic factors for long-term outcome in patients with anti-NMDA receptor encephalitis: an observational cohort study.
evidence.snippet: Treatment included first-line immunotherapy (steroids, intravenous immunoglobulin, plasmapheresis), second-line immunotherapy (rituximab, cyclophosphamide), and tumour removal.

evidence.reference_title: Treatment and prognostic factors for long-term outcome in patients with anti-NMDA receptor encephalitis: an observational cohort study.
evidence.snippet: 472 (94%) underwent first-line immunotherapy or tumour removal, resulting in improvement within 4 weeks in 251 (53%).

nodes.description: Loss of synaptic NMDA receptors disrupts glutamatergic signaling and synaptic plasticity, providing a mechanistic bridge to psychiatric, cognitive, seizure, movement, autonomic, and ventilatory manifestations.

evidence.reference_title: Anti-NMDAR antibodies, the blood-brain barrier, and anti-NMDAR encephalitis.
evidence.snippet: Recent research suggests that anti-NMDAR antibodies may reduce NMDAR levels in this disorder, compromising synaptic activity in the hippocampus.

evidence.reference_title: Anti-NMDA-receptor encephalitis: case series and analysis of the effects of antibodies.
evidence.snippet: This feature is compatible with disruption of the mechanisms of synaptic plasticity, thought to underlie learning and memory, in which the NMDA receptors play a key part.

nodes.description: Corticosteroids are commonly used in first-line acute immunotherapy, including pulsed intravenous methylprednisolone in severe disease.

evidence.reference_title: Anti-NMDAR encephalitis: A single-center, longitudinal study in China.
evidence.snippet: A total of 208 (94.5%) patients received steroids, of whom 103 (46.8%) received pulsed IV methylprednisolone.

nodes.description: Antibody production is concentrated in the CNS compartment: CSF antibody levels can exceed serum levels even when the blood-brain barrier is preserved, supporting intrathecal synthesis by antibody-secreting cells.

evidence.reference_title: Anti-NMDA-receptor encephalitis: case series and analysis of the effects of antibodies.
evidence.snippet: Analysis of normalized concentrations of IgG showed that all 53 patients had higher concentrations of antibodies in CSF than in sera, indicating intrathecal synthesis of antibodies (figure 3).

evidence.reference_title: Anti-NMDAR antibodies, the blood-brain barrier, and anti-NMDAR encephalitis.
evidence.snippet: It is characterized by the existence of antibodies against NMDAR, mainly against the GluN1 subunit, in cerebrospinal fluid (CSF).

nodes.description: Intravenous immunoglobulin is a first-line immunotherapy used alone or with corticosteroids and/or plasma exchange.

evidence.reference_title: Anti-NMDAR encephalitis: A single-center, longitudinal study in China.
evidence.snippet: IVIG was administered to 199 (90.5%) patients, and 7 (3.2%) patients underwent PE.

nodes.description: Mycophenolate mofetil and azathioprine are used as longer-term immunosuppressive treatment in selected severe, refractory, or relapsing anti-NMDAR encephalitis cases.

evidence.reference_title: Anti-NMDAR encephalitis: A single-center, longitudinal study in China.
evidence.snippet: Long-term immunotherapy was administered mainly in patients who were enrolled later, as an add-on therapy for severe or refractory patients in the acute phase, or as maintenance therapy to prevent and manage relapses.

evidence.reference_title: Anti-NMDAR encephalitis: A single-center, longitudinal study in China.
evidence.snippet: In general, MMF was administered in 109 (49.5%) patients, 55 of whom at onset and 54 after relapse, and AZA was administered in 8 (3.6%) patients.

nodes.description: The downstream clinical syndrome reflects reversible NMDAR hypofunction and encephalitic network dysfunction, with psychiatric symptoms and seizures most often reported in large cohorts and additional movement, consciousness, autonomic, and ventilatory involvement in severe disease.

evidence.reference_title: An update on anti-NMDA receptor encephalitis for neurologists and psychiatrists: mechanisms and models.
evidence.snippet: The identification of anti-NMDA receptor (NMDAR) encephalitis about 12 years ago made it possible to recognise that some patients with rapidly progressive psychiatric symptoms or cognitive impairment, seizures, abnormal movements, or coma of unknown cause, had an autoimmune disease.

nodes.description: Plasma exchange is used as an acute first-line or escalation strategy to remove pathogenic antibodies.

evidence.reference_title: Treatment and prognostic factors for long-term outcome in patients with anti-NMDA receptor encephalitis: an observational cohort study.
evidence.snippet: Treatment included first-line immunotherapy (steroids, intravenous immunoglobulin, plasmapheresis), second-line immunotherapy (rituximab, cyclophosphamide), and tumour removal.

nodes.description: Patients who fail first-line therapy may receive rituximab and/or cyclophosphamide as second-line immunotherapy.

evidence.reference_title: Treatment and prognostic factors for long-term outcome in patients with anti-NMDA receptor encephalitis: an observational cohort study.
evidence.snippet: Of 221 patients who did not improve with first-line treatment, 125 (57%) received second-line immunotherapy that resulted in a better outcome (mRS 0-2) than those who did not

nodes.description: Patient IgG binds neuronal surface NMDA receptors and reduces the cell-surface and postsynaptic receptor cluster pool, producing a selective and potentially reversible receptor loss rather than fixed neuronal death.

evidence.reference_title: Anti-NMDA-receptor encephalitis: case series and analysis of the effects of antibodies.
evidence.snippet: adding patients’ IgG to rat hippocampal neuronal cultures produced a concentration-dependent decrease of the cell-surface fraction of NMDA receptors (figure 5).

evidence.reference_title: Anti-NMDA-receptor encephalitis: case series and analysis of the effects of antibodies.
evidence.snippet: Together, these findings show that patients’ antibodies produce a selective and reversible decrease of NMDA-receptor clusters in postsynaptic dendrites.

nodes.description: Tumor antigen exposure, particularly from ovarian teratoma, or post-infectious inflammation after herpes simplex encephalitis can trigger B-cell autoimmunity against extracellular NMDA receptor epitopes.

evidence.reference_title: An update on anti-NMDA receptor encephalitis for neurologists and psychiatrists: mechanisms and models.
evidence.snippet: Tumours, usually ovarian teratoma, and herpes simplex encephalitis are known triggers of NMDAR autoimmunity.

evidence.reference_title: Anti-NMDA-receptor encephalitis: case series and analysis of the effects of antibodies.
evidence.snippet: All teratomas contained nervous tissue; 25 were examined for expression of NMDA receptors, and all were positive (data not shown).

nodes.description: Surgical removal of an associated ovarian teratoma or other tumor is part of standard management when a tumor is identified.

evidence.reference_title: An update on anti-NMDA receptor encephalitis for neurologists and psychiatrists: mechanisms and models.
evidence.snippet: About 80% of patients improve with immunotherapy and, if needed, tumour removal, but the recovery is slow.
